MINI MINUTES:

Ronald D. Foreman, Esq. of Foreman & Brasso (415.433.3475) and Joseph M. Alioto of the Alioto Law Firm (415.434.8900) appearing for Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant / Opposing Party David Greene;
Andrew M. Zacks, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman (415.956.8100) appearing for Cross-Complainant /
Moving Party Park Lane Associates LP.

RULING - A R G U E D: The Court, having reviewed the briefs submitted and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as modified: CROSS COMPLAINANT PARK LANE ASSOCIATES LP's DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to allege facts in support of each affirmative defense. Opposing party cites no authority for the proposition that the sufficiency of allegation in the answer to cross-complaint may be evaluated based on supplementing it by allegations contained in the complaint. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, the fact that a party seeking declaratory relief is in pari delicto is a consideration which may be considered by the court as a sufficient reason for declining to grant declaratory relief. Moss v. Moss (1942) 20 Cal.2d 640. Order signed in open court. Judge: Lillian K. Sing; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (D501)
